title: sedum acre subsp. sexangulare
intro: Sedum acre subsp. sexangulare is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae, commonly known as the stonecrop. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, characterized by its hexagonal-shaped leaves and vibrant yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Sedum acre subsp. sexangulare, such as teas and infusions, have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections.
